title: How Mom and Dad Fell in Love (According to Them)

sentences.en: Thursday. Today is Mom and Dad's wedding anniversary, and every year on this date, they retell the story of how they met, with small new details added each time.
sentences.zh: 星期四。今天是媽媽和爸爸的結婚紀念日，每年到了這一天，他們都會重講一次相遇的故事，而且每次都會多加一些新細節。

sentences.en: According to Dad, he was a humble, low-level clerk at a small company, filing papers nobody else wanted to touch.
sentences.zh: 根據爸爸的說法，他當時只是一位謙遜的低階職員，在一間小公司負責整理沒有人想碰的文件。

sentences.en: Mom, meanwhile, was a curious young woman visiting that same office to deliver a package for her own boss, who was too busy to go herself.
sentences.zh: 同時，媽媽當時是一位好奇心旺盛的年輕女子，她去那間辦公室送一個包裹，是幫她自己的主管跑腿，因為主管太忙不能親自去。

sentences.en: "I accidentally called her by the wrong name," Dad admitted, "which, looking back, was an extremely rude way to start a conversation."
sentences.zh: 「我不小心叫錯了她的名字，」爸爸坦承：「現在回想起來，這實在是個很沒禮貌的開場方式。」

sentences.en: Mom laughed and said she was too shy at the time to correct him properly, so she just quietly answered to the wrong name for ten whole minutes.
sentences.zh: 媽媽笑著說，她當時太害羞，不好意思糾正他，所以就安安靜靜地答應了整整十分鐘那個叫錯的名字。

sentences.en: "He seemed like such a gentleman otherwise," she said, "clumsy, but genuinely kind, which honestly matters more in the long run."
sentences.zh: 「除此之外他看起來還滿紳士的，」她說：「笨手笨腳，但真心善良，長遠來看，這其實更重要。」

sentences.en: They ran into each other again a few weeks later, and this time Dad remembered her actual name, calling it "a small but important step forward."
sentences.zh: 幾個星期後他們又碰面了，這次爸爸記住了她真正的名字，他說這是「一個雖小但很重要的進步」。

sentences.en: Sophia, who has heard this story roughly one hundred times, still pretends to be surprised at the exact same part every single year.
sentences.zh: Sophia 已經聽過這個故事大概一百次了，每年還是假裝在同一個橋段感到驚訝。

sentences.en: "Marriage takes more than one lucky meeting," she added, cutting into the anniversary cake with unusual precision. "It takes years of choosing each other, again and again."
sentences.zh: 「婚姻靠的不只是一次幸運的相遇，」她一邊補充，一邊異常精準地切著紀念蛋糕：「靠的是一年又一年、一次又一次選擇彼此。」

sentences.en: William asked what a husband and a wife actually promise each other, and Dad said, simply, to stay kind on the difficult days, not just the easy ones.
sentences.zh: William 問丈夫和妻子到底彼此承諾了什麼，爸爸簡單地說：在困難的日子裡也要保持善良，不只是在輕鬆的日子裡。

sentences.en: This year's dinner also included my aunt and uncle, along with their two kids, meaning I officially have one more nephew and one niece to keep track of.
sentences.zh: 今年的晚餐還多了我阿姨和姨丈，加上他們兩個孩子，代表我現在多了一位姪子和一位姪女要記得。

sentences.en: My little niece kept asking Mom to retell the "wrong name" part over and over, clearly the most popular scene in the entire family legend.
sentences.zh: 我的小姪女一直要求媽媽把「叫錯名字」那段一講再講，顯然這是整個家族傳說裡最受歡迎的一幕。

sentences.en: My nephew, older and far more mature than William, mostly just wanted extra cake, which is honestly a very mature goal to have.
sentences.zh: 我的姪子年紀比較大，也比 William 成熟很多，他大部分時候只想多要一塊蛋糕，老實說，這也算是個滿成熟的目標。

sentences.en: By the end of dinner, Mom and Dad had told the story exactly the same way they always do, wrong name, rude first impression, and all.
sentences.zh: 晚餐結束時，媽媽和爸爸還是照著他們一貫的方式講完了整個故事，叫錯名字、無禮的第一印象，全部一樣。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: apparently the secret to a good marriage is one accidental wrong name, a lot of patience, and repeating the same story every single year with a smile.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：顯然一段好婚姻的秘訣，就是一次意外叫錯的名字、大量的耐心，還有每年都帶著笑容重講同一個故事。

quiz.question: How does Dad first make a bad impression on Mom?
options: He accidentally calls her by the wrong name.
He arrives very late to meet her.

quiz.question: According to Sophia, what does marriage really take?
options: Years of choosing each other, again and again.
One lucky meeting and nothing else.

quiz.question: Who joins the anniversary dinner this year, adding a nephew and niece to Coco's family?
options: Coco's aunt and uncle with their two kids.
